text:
User: ok, make it selectable -- let's unify:

remove mgmt session and pty

make it tenex-edge sessions

which shows an inquirer tui that allows me to select to kill, attach -- no need for "inject", no need for "resize" features (at least I don't have that need -- don't know if that stuff is used by something internal; hopefully not [...] - Remove public `mgmt session` and `pty` commands entirely—no aliases. [...] - `tenex-edge sessions` is human-only; old `pty` and `mgmt session` trees are removed. [...] - `tenex-edge sessions` opens the existing rich fuzzy session picker.
- Select one session, then choose Attach or Kill. [...] Assistant: The schema-4 daemon is live on PID 90518, all 44 supervisors are still present, agent help hides `sessions`, and human help exposes it while the removed command trees reject. I’m doing the final interactive smoke now—opening the real picker in a TTY and exiting without touching any session. [...] which shows an inquirer tui that allows me to select to kill, attach -- no need for "inject", no need for "resize" features (at least I don't have that need -- don't know if that stuff is used by something internal; hopefully not [...] - Remove public Inject/Resize actions, but retain their internal mechanisms: injection powers mention delivery and prompts; resize is part of terminal attachment. [...] Assistant: The operator surface is now top-level `sessions`; the old public `mgmt session` and `pty` command trees are removed, while the supervisor and attach/inject/resize internals remain available to the runtime. I’ve also switched the daemon projection to canonical workspace-grouped channels and the picker to fixed two-line, single-cursor rows. Next I’m wiring the destructive action through the ownership layers so Shift+K expires presence and confirms channel removal immediately.
